title: Don't bother with --no-pivot for rootless isolation
body:
When running outside of a container, --no-pivot isn't necessary, and when running inside of a container, it's not enough to solve any of the difficulties we're seeing there.  It may trigger an EPERM for unshare()
calls inside of the container that we launch, and we don't want that, so drop it, for now at least.

comments.body: Is there a reason to use this Option?
I had thought it was be necessary for running `runc` inside of a container.  It might still be necessary, but it isn't sufficient AFAICT.

yes I agree, I think it is not required for buildah.  I was surprised when I saw it used (and had to implement it for crun to keep it usable with Buildah: https://github.com/giuseppe/crun/commit/d32bf5cb57cb10be5703ca5f3d5336da897fc730)

LGTM
